name: Troels Holger Vaaben
tagline: Cancer • Microbiome • Synthetic Biology • Translational Research

paragraphs: I’m a bioinformatician and microbial genomics researcher working at the intersection of metagenomics, pangenomics, and multi-omics integration to understand host–microbe interactions in infectious disease and cancer. I care about analyses that are both rigorous and useful: from careful study design and data generation to interpretable models, clear biological narratives, and reproducible deliverables.

My work is centered on data-driven microbiome research, with a particular focus on metagenomics, pangenomics, transcriptomics, metabolomics, and multi-omics integration in translational and disease-oriented settings. I am interested in building analyses and workflows that connect microbial composition, functional potential, and host response in a way that is biologically interpretable and useful for real projects. If you are working on data-driven microbiome research and would like to collaborate, feel free to get in touch.
